# Symbol Index as Always-Fresh LSP/ctags Artifact

## Summary

**One-sentence:** Maintain a committed, always-fresh symbol index of the codebase — generated by an LSP-based tool (Serena, SourceGraph, or ctags --languages=.

**One-paragraph:** Maintain a committed, always-fresh symbol index of the codebase — generated by an LSP-based tool (Serena, SourceGraph, or ctags --languages=... with the universal-ctags tags-from-lsp workflow) — and expose it to coding agents as the canonical "where is this defined / where is this used" lookup. The index is rebuilt by a pre-commit hook + a CI job that fails when the committed index drifts from the live AST. Agents are instructed (in the project AGENTS.md) to consult the symbol index for definition / reference / call-graph queries before falling back to text grep, cutting context cost by 5-20x on large repos.

## Applies If (ALL must hold)

- Any monorepo over ~50k LOC where text grep blows the agent's context window.
- Polyglot repos where one tool (universal-ctags / Serena) covers every language uniformly.
- Sandboxed agent runtimes (Docker exec, GitHub Actions runners) where bringing up an LSP server is impractical.
- Refactor-heavy work where call-graph queries replace bulk file reads.

## Skip If (ANY kills it)

- Single-file scripts or notebooks — index overhead dominates.
- Repos where the LSP server is already always-on in the agent runtime (Cursor desktop) and a local index just adds drift risk.
- Vendored / read-only dirs — index those once and exclude from drift checks.
